Package org.key_project.logic

Interface Term

All Superinterfaces:
LogicElement, Sorted, SyntaxElement
All Known Subinterfaces:
Term
public interface Term extends LogicElement, Sorted
This interface is implemented by classes that represent terms or formulas.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    int
    arity()
    The arity of the term.
    ImmutableArray<? extends QuantifiableVariable>
    boundVars()
    The logical variables bound by the top level operator.
    int
    depth()
    The nesting depth of this term.
    <T extends Term>
    void
    execPostOrder(Visitor<T> visitor)
    The visitor is handed through till the bottom of the tree, and then it walks upwards, while at each upstep the method visit of the visitor is called.
    <T extends Term>
    void
    execPreOrder(Visitor<T> visitor)
    The visitor walks downwards the tree, while at each downstep the method visit of the visitor is called.
    ImmutableSet<? extends QuantifiableVariable>
    freeVars()
    The set of free quantifiable variables occurring in this term.
    default SyntaxElement
    getChild(int n)
    Get the n-th child of this syntax element.
    default int
    getChildCount()
     
    boolean
    isRigid()
    Whether all operators in this term are rigid.
    Operator
    op()
    The top operator (e.g., in "A and B" this is "and", in f(x,y) it is "f").
    <T> T
    op(Class<T> opClass)
    The top operator (e.g., in "A and B" this is "and", in f(x,y) it is "f") cast to the passed type.
    int
    serialNumber()
    Returns a serial number for a term.
    Sort
    sort()
    The sort of the term.
    Term
    sub(int n)
    The n-th direct subterm.
    ImmutableArray<? extends Term>
    subs()
    The subterms.
    ImmutableArray<? extends QuantifiableVariable>
    varsBoundHere(int n)
    The logical variables bound by the top level operator for the nth subterm.

    Methods inherited from interface org.key_project.logic.SyntaxElement

    getCursor

  • Method Details

    • op

      Operator op()
      The top operator (e.g., in "A and B" this is "and", in f(x,y) it is "f").

    • op

      <T> T op(Class<T> opClass) throws IllegalArgumentException
      The top operator (e.g., in "A and B" this is "and", in f(x,y) it is "f") cast to the passed type.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException

      The subterms.

      The logical variables bound by the top level operator.

      The logical variables bound by the top level operator for the nth subterm.

      The arity of the term.

      The sort of the term.
      Specified by:
      sort in interface Sorted
      Returns:
      the Sort of the sorted entity

    • depth

      int depth()
      The nesting depth of this term.

    • isRigid

      boolean isRigid()
      Whether all operators in this term are rigid.

    • freeVars

      ImmutableSet<? extends QuantifiableVariable> freeVars()
      The set of free quantifiable variables occurring in this term.

    • serialNumber

      int serialNumber()
      Returns a serial number for a term. The serial number is not persistent.

    • execPostOrder

      <T extends Term> void execPostOrder(Visitor<T> visitor)
      The visitor is handed through till the bottom of the tree, and then it walks upwards, while at each upstep the method visit of the visitor is called.
      Parameters:
      visitor - the Visitor

    • execPreOrder

      <T extends Term> void execPreOrder(Visitor<T> visitor)
      The visitor walks downwards the tree, while at each downstep the method visit of the visitor is called.
      Parameters:
      visitor - the Visitor

    • getChildCount

      default int getChildCount()
      Specified by:
      getChildCount in interface SyntaxElement
      Returns:
      the number of children of this syntax element.

    • getChild

      default SyntaxElement getChild(int n)
      Description copied from interface: SyntaxElement
      Get the n-th child of this syntax element.
      Specified by:
      getChild in interface SyntaxElement
      Parameters:
      n - index of the child.
      Returns:
      the n-th child of this syntax element.
